title: Paspalum ctenostachyum
taxonomy: Paspalum ctenostachyum is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is classified under the subfamily Panicoideae and the tribe Paniceae. Further classification places it in the genus Paspalum and the subgenus Paspalum. The species is native to tropical America. It is part of the large and diverse family of grasses, with over 12,000 known species.
uses: Paspalum ctenostachyum is a type of grass known to have various medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, its extracts are used to treat various ailments including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Additionally, Paspalum ctenostachyum is used in folk medicine to treat digestive issues and as a natural remedy for wounds. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits.

intro: Paspalum ctenostachyum, also known as Brazilian pennsylvania grass or Paspalum, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Paspalum ctenostachyum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Paspalum ctenostachyum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
